projects.title: Terrain Gurzelen Vol. 1
projects.description:
Terrain Gurzelen Vol. 1 is an 860-page multilingual volume (French, German, and English) that documents, through texts and images, the temporary use of the former football stadium in Biel/Bienne (CH). Built in 1950, closed in 2015, and slated for demolition, the stadium was reopened in 2017 when the city entrusted its management to an association for a temporary use project.

Spanning an area of over 24,000 m2, more than 60 social, artistic, and cultural initiatives took place, using the space as a site for experimentation and gathering.

The book brings together over 250 contributions, including texts, poems, essays, visual works, photographs, and archival materials. The volume combines documentation, testimonies, and visual materials related to this collective experience.



curated by Roman Luterbacher, Marc Tadorian and Emmanuel Crivelli (Dual Room)

projects.title: AG Fronzoni. Il progetto tra bianco e nero
projects.description:
AG Fronzoni Il progetto tra bianco nero is a monographic research about the italian graphic designer AG Fronzoni. Thanks to the access to different archives we were able to collect and photograph part of his work in its original form.

The book is mainly divided into two:
documents→ A collection of essays and interviews around the figure of AGF.

projects→ Containing posters, editorial and branding works, product design and architecture projects.

The last pages of the book, highlighted by the choice to use a yellow paper, contain the text Grafica Italiana dal 1945 a oggi by Carlo Vinti, an historical research around the most recent graphic scene in Italy. To better access the great quantity of names cited in this text we developed a system that allows the reader to easily find them in the page.



w/ Noa Merlini, Agnese Pozzobon

projects.title: What’s the opposite of precarity? Progettualità condivise e collettività istantanee
projects.description:
This research stems from a personal and political affinity with collective practices, explored as a possible response to precarity. Starting from the question What’s the opposite of precarity?, it investigates forms of care, sharing, and dissent that challenge individualistic approaches to design.

The process led to the development of a residency: an in-between space-time addressed to young creatives navigating a transitional phase between education and professionalization, where collectivity can be experienced as a viable alternative and as a tool for orientation.



master thesis w/ Agnese Pozzobon 
supervised by Nicola Chemotti

projects.title: 10JA Exhibition – Dimitri Bähler
projects.description:
The 10JA project began at the end of 2023 with the idea of celebrating the tenth anniversary of Dimitri Bähler’s studio while simultaneously creating an archive. The exhibition brought together a wide range of works, from serially produced pieces to unique objects, projects developed over the course of a week or several years, as well as both old and new prototypes.

Presented across an entire floor above the studio, the exhibition opened on November 29, 2024, and ran for three weeks, accompanied by various events. The scenography draws inspiration from the building’s former tenant, the Flüeli Printing Company, which closed in the 1990s, and makes use of around two thousand leftover sheets of paper, in four shades of white. These were arranged on a custom-built structure to create six main spaces, optimizing the display of the objects.

The same paper used as walls was later reused to print the exhibition catalogue.

projects.title: Ci sono muri troppo alti per te
projects.description:
This photographic project focuses on the Cretto of Alberto Burri, a monumental land art work built over the ruins of Gibellina in Sicily, one of the towns destroyed by the 1968 Belìce earthquake. Through a series of images, the book traces the complex relationship between memory, landscape, and reconstruction. Rather than simply documenting the site, the project reflects on how the Cretto functions as both a memorial and a space of suspension, a visible imprint of collective trauma and abandonment.

By combining photography with historical context, Ci sono muri troppo alti per te offers a visual exploration of the lasting impact of the earthquake and the role of art in shaping public memory.

projects.title: Metamorfosi
projects.description:
Metamorfosi is a variable typeface inspired by the plant principle of metamorphosis. As an incipit to the adaptation methods of the plant world, in this case thorns, the typeface has the possibility of being used without or with prunes, being able to vary in length and extrusion.



w/ Agnese Pozzobon, Nikolai V. Martini

projects.title: Come fare tipografia svizzera?
projects.description:
This book contains the text of Wolfgang Weingart’s lessons about typography, held during the 70s in the United States. W. Weingart’s experimental and heretical approach to traditional graphic design principles unquestionably led to a significant turning point in the international scene.

Embodying his design action, the adjective swiss is reversed throughout the entire book. This operation is further amplified by the transparency of the paper and the alternating of printed and unprinted pages, which leave the possibility of observing the back of the typeface. Weingart’s approach in treating and handling typeface as an object with its own three-dimensionality is reinterpreted in this way, as in his well-known Round Compositions, where he imprints the back of the lead typeface on the paper.



w/ Noa Merlini, Agnese Pozzobon

projects.title: radio spugna
projects.description:
The project started within the academic institution of ISIA in Urbino, a protected but confined area. It is from this delimited location that we use radio to project ourselves outwards and bridge places and people far apart in space through the connections that words can generate.

radio spugna’s manifesto is rooted in five principles:

    Sponge
The sponge is both a porous and relational element.
    Precariousness
Depending by what the sponge comes in contact with, it varies its colour and volume: in such variable nature we recognise a highly precarious form, reflecting our own phase of existence and of our generation.
    Transience
We value the cyclical, undefined, ever-changing and what is capable of being resorbed back into the whole.
    Radio
We believe that the radio is a powerful social and cultural device, able to position itself and act between the public and private spheres, making it the ideal medium to express ourselves.
    Chorality
As a porous space, the sponge is by nature subjected to contamination. Our aim is to create an inclusive and collaborative broadcasting space, welcoming diverse voices and fostering a collective participation in its creation.



on-going project w/ Roberta Antinolfi, Noa Merlini, Agnese Pozzobon, Nikolai V. Martini
